title: Resume a source-grounded comparison after a weak draft
goal: Compare two memory strategies and keep every claim tied to a source.
interruption_point: The run stopped after a weak draft and a better structure plan.

events.text: Goal: Compare two memory strategies and keep every claim tied to a source.
Plan: Read both notes, pull their strongest claims, organize them into an evidence matrix, and only then write the recommendation.
Constraint: Do not write the final recommendation without explicit supporting evidence.
Source: docs/memory_strategy_a.md says a goal reminder should appear before broader retrieval.
Attempt: Drafted the recommendation from memory before building the matrix. The draft was weak.
Lesson: The comparison will stay weak unless each claim points to a source row in the matrix.
Decision: Build the evidence matrix first and defer the recommendation.
Next step: Add a row for each claim, source, and open question before writing prose.
